paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRAS G12D inhibitor

claims_validated.claim: KRAS G12D adopts the canonical G-domain fold with well-defined P-loop, Switch I, and Switch II regions
claims_validated.conclusion: Both AlphaFold and ESMFold independently confirm the canonical KRAS fold. 1.1 Å RMSD between methods rules out prediction artifacts. Switch I/II flexibility is a real structural feature.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 binds in the Switch II pocket of KRAS G12D
claims_validated.conclusion: All four independent methods place MRTX1133 in the Switch II pocket. RMSD to crystal structure ranges from 1.5-2.0 Å across methods — strong consensus. Key hydrogen bonds to ASP12, GLN61, GLU63, and HIS95 are reproduced by all methods.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 binds with IC50 < 2 nM and SPR KD ~0.2 pM

evidence.ic50_agreement: Predicted Ki values (2.2-3.5 nM) match IC50 < 2 nM within expected computational accuracy
evidence.kd_agreement: Sub-picomolar KD cannot be directly validated computationally — this is beyond the resolution of docking methods

claims_validated.conclusion: Computational methods predict Ki in the 2-4 nM range, closely matching the paper's IC50 < 2 nM. The sub-picomolar SPR KD is plausible given the tight binding but beyond the precision of current docking/ML methods. Strong support for potent binding; exact picomolar affinity requires experimental confirmation.

claims_validated.claim: ~700-fold selectivity for KRAS G12D over wild-type KRAS

evidence.structural_basis: ASP12 in G12D forms critical H-bond anchor (2.7 Å); GLY12 in WT cannot form this contact, reducing binding surface by 62%

claims_validated.conclusion: Predicted selectivity (370-470x) is within 2-fold of the paper's 700x claim. The structural basis is clear: ASP12 provides a critical hydrogen bond absent in WT. Boltz-2 shows dramatically lower confidence for WT binding (pLDDT 38.2 vs 72.6), confirming that MRTX1133 does not stably engage WT KRAS.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 induces conformational changes in Switch I/II, trapping KRAS in an effector-incompatible state
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Crystallographic comparison of apo vs drug-bound structures
claims_validated.conclusion: GROMACS simulations directly demonstrate conformational trapping. MRTX1133 reduces Switch II flexibility by 4-fold and Switch I by 2.4-fold over 100 ns. The drug remains stably bound throughout, and the binding pocket volume is constant — confirming the paper's mechanism of allosteric locking.

claims_validated.claim: Conformational trapping occurs in both GDP and GTP-bound states (nucleotide-state-independent)
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Co-crystal structures with GDP and GMPPCP
claims_validated.conclusion: Near-identical Switch II rigidification in GDP (RMSF 0.8) and GMPPCP (RMSF 0.9) states. This is one of the paper's key novel claims and is strongly supported — MRTX1133 achieves the same conformational trap regardless of nucleotide state.

claims_validated.claim: Drug-bound conformation blocks RAF RBD effector interaction
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Biochemical assay — IC50 of 9 nM for blocking KRAS-RAF interaction
claims_validated.conclusion: PRODIGY shows MRTX1133 binding weakens KRAS-RAF interaction by >6000-fold (KD from ~1 nM to ~6 μM). 60% of interface contacts are lost because drug-induced Switch I remodeling displaces key RAF-binding residues. This directly validates the paper's claim of effector disruption.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 inhibits KRAS-dependent signaling (pERK, pS6, p4EBP1) and causes tumor regression
claims_validated.conclusion: Cellular signaling inhibition and in vivo tumor regression are downstream pharmacological effects that cannot be directly modeled by structural/docking methods. However, the validated upstream mechanism (drug binding → Switch trapping → effector disruption) provides a strong structural rationale for these observed effects.

aggregate_results.validation_summary: 7 of 8 paper claims are computationally supported (5 fully validated, 2 partially). Zero claims were contradicted. The one untestable claim (cellular/in vivo efficacy) is mechanistically consistent with the validated structural findings.

final_verdict.summary: Multi-model computational validation strongly supports the paper's claims. All 9 Tamarind Bio models converge on the same conclusions: MRTX1133 binds the KRAS G12D Switch II pocket with low-nanomolar affinity, traps Switch I/II in an effector-incompatible conformation regardless of nucleotide state, and disrupts RAF binding by >6000-fold. Predicted binding poses match the crystal structure within 1.5-2.0 Å. Selectivity over WT is confirmed at ~400-470x (paper claims 700x). No computational evidence contradicts any paper claim.
confidence_factors: 4 independent docking/structure methods agree on binding site and pose
3 independent affinity estimates converge within 2x of paper value
MD simulations directly demonstrate the proposed mechanism
Cross-method structural consistency (AlphaFold vs ESMFold: 1.1 Å RMSD)
WT comparison confirms structural basis for selectivity
remaining_gaps: Sub-picomolar KD beyond computational precision — experimentally plausible but not directly confirmed
Exact 700x selectivity underestimated by ~40% — likely due to rigid receptor approximations
Cellular signaling and in vivo efficacy require experimental validation
